description: Phase 2: pro redesign + features for the geotag camera. Navy+gold brand. Remove capture friction, redesign GPS card / mode selector / camera controls / nav, add weather, layout presets, WYSIWYG stamp, altitude+compass, EXIF GPS, home dashboard, photo grid, timestamp/watermark, pro gestures. Follow tasks/prd-gpstools-phase2.md + 06-ui-redesign-v2-spec.md, substituting navy #15294D + gold #F2A93B for the spec's blue.

userStories.title: Navy+gold brand theme
userStories.description: As a user, I want a consistent premium navy+gold look.
acceptanceCriteria: Material3 theme: navy #15294D primary + gold #F2A93B accent, light/dark safe
GPS accuracy colour tokens: green #34A853, amber #FB8C00, red #E53935
Existing screens adopt new colours; no leftover green/blue placeholders
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify new theme on emulator
userStories.notes: Done: Color.kt navy+gold + accuracy tokens (accuracyColor helper), Theme.kt light(navy primary)/dark(gold primary) schemes, dynamicColor off. No hardcoded brand colors elsewhere; screens consume colorScheme. Verified navy nav pill on emulator.

userStories.title: One-tap capture (remove forced Stamp details dialog)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to tap and capture without a blocking dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: Shutter captures immediately; no blocking Stamp details modal
Stamp auto-fills address, coords, date/time (and weather when available)
Last-used project/site name + note apply silently if set
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify no forced dialog on capture on emulator
userStories.notes: Shutter in CameraPreview already fires capturePhoto directly — no Stamp-details modal ever blocks the shutter (the CustomFieldsDialog opens only via the Edit affordance). Stamp auto-fills address/coords/date-time from locationState snapshot; last-used project/site name + note now re-read from CustomFieldsStore.load() AT shutter time (cheap SharedPreferences read, consistent with AppSettingsStore inline reads) so they always apply silently. Weather is US-009. Verified on emulator: tapping shutter saved GPS_20260616_105606130.jpg with no dialog.

userStories.title: Optional stamp-details bottom sheet
userStories.description: As a field user, I want to optionally add project/site/note/logo.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit affordance (icon + 'Edit' text) on the GPS card opens a bottom sheet
Fields: project/site name, note, logo; persisted; never blocks capture
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify bottom sheet open/save/persist on emulator
userStories.notes: Edit affordance (pencil icon + 'Edit'/संपादित करें text) now lives ON the GPS card (LocationInfoOverlay gained an optional onEditClick param + EditAffordance composable); removed the old free-floating top-right IconButton. CustomFieldsDialog (AlertDialog) converted to CustomFieldsSheet (ModalBottomSheet, @OptIn ExperimentalMaterial3Api) — slides up, dismissible, never blocks capture; project/note committed on Save (gold Button), logo add/remove immediate. Persists via existing CustomFieldsStore. Verified on emulator: tapped Edit on card → sheet opened pre-filled with persisted 'Site-42-Bridg' → Save dismissed back to live camera.

userStories.title: Top GPS card redesign + remove duplicate block
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean readable info card with one clean stamp.
acceptanceCriteria: Hierarchy: address bold white 16sp, plus code + pincode 14sp 90% white, coords 12sp grey
Accuracy chip colour-coded: green Good <10m / amber Avg 10-20m / red Poor >20m
Card bg black 90%, 16dp corners, 12dp padding
Remove redundant filename/dimensions block from photo/gallery overlay
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify card + chip + single clean stamp on emulator
userStories.notes: GPS card redesigned in LocationInfoOverlay.kt: 3-tier hierarchy (address bold 16sp white / plus code 14sp 90% white / coords 12sp grey #9AA0A6). Plus code computed locally via new encodePlusCode() in LocationProvider.kt (standard 10-digit OLC, no dep, always shows even when reverse-geocode is down on emulator). New AccuracyChip composable: colour-coded pill (accuracyColor token) Good<10m green / Avg 10-20m amber / Poor>20m red, label '±Nm · Good'. Card bg black 0.9, 16dp corners, 12dp padding. Removed redundant dimensions line from GalleryScreen PhotoMetadata. New strings accuracy_chip/good/avg/poor in both values + values-hi. Verified on emulator: card shows MP89X26F+2G + green '±5मी · अच्छा' chip at geo 30.132034,78.321448.

userStories.title: Mode selector redesign + Pro badge
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear mode chips with a sensible Pro indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: Centered chips with icons: Classic, Minimal, Field Report
Selected = navy/gold fill + white text; unselected = transparent + grey #9AA0A6
Replace Field Report lock with a small gold 'PRO' badge; keep it usable (unlocked for now)
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify selected state + PRO badge on emulator
userStories.notes: TemplatePickerRow in CameraPreview.kt redesigned: centered (fillMaxWidth + Arrangement.spacedBy(8dp, CenterHorizontally), dropped horizontalScroll) icon FilterChips — Classic=PhotoCamera, Minimal=CenterFocusStrong, Field Report=Description (StampTemplate.icon() helper). Selected = BrandNavy fill + white label/icon; unselected = transparent + grey TextSecondary #9AA0A6, border=null. Lock replaced by gold ProBadge (BrandGold bg, navy 'PRO' text, RoundedCornerShape) shown when premium && !isPremium. Field Report now SELECTABLE/usable: dropped onLocked/billing purchase routing + the capture premium-downgrade fallback (uses selected template as-is). New pro_badge string (both values+values-hi). Verified on emulator: tapping Field Report moves navy selection to it (no purchase dialog), PRO badge stays.

userStories.title: Camera controls redesign
userStories.description: As a user, I want pro camera controls with flash.
acceptanceCriteria: Shutter ~72dp, white fill, ring + 8dp elevation
Add Flash toggle (off/on/auto); keep flip-camera; side buttons 48dp grey 20%
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify shutter + flash + flip on emulator
userStories.notes: Camera controls rebuilt as a single bottom Row (flash | shutter | flip) in CameraPreview.kt. Shutter = Material3 Surface(onClick) 72dp, white fill, 4dp LightGray ring border, 8dp shadowElevation, CircleShape (replaced FilledIconButton+Lens icon); contentDescription via Modifier.semantics (pre-resolved string, since Surface content lambda isn't @Composable for stringResource). New SideControlButton composable = 48dp FilledIconButton, white@0.2 (grey 20%) container, white icon — used for both flash and flip. Flash toggle cycles off→on→auto via nextFlashMode(); flashIcon() maps to FlashOff/FlashOn/FlashAuto; applied to the shared ImageCapture via LaunchedEffect(flashMode){imageCapture.flashMode=...} + rememberSaveable flashMode. Flip kept (Cameraswitch); shown only when both lenses exist, else a 48dp Spacer keeps the shutter centered (Arrangement.SpaceBetween). Removed the old free-floating BottomEnd flip IconButton + Lens/IconButton imports. New strings camera_flash/_off/_on/_auto (both values+values-hi). Verified on emulator: row renders flash(left)/white-ring shutter(center)/flip(right); tapping flash flipped the icon FlashOff→FlashOn (cycle works).

userStories.title: Bottom nav polish + map badge
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean nav and a map count badge.
acceptanceCriteria: Selected tab navy/gold + bold; height 64dp; labels 11sp
Map tab badge with count of location-tagged photos (hidden when 0)
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify nav styling + badge on emulator
userStories.notes: BottomNavBar in MainActivity.kt: NavigationBar height 64dp; labels 11sp, FontWeight.Bold when selected; selected icon/text = colorScheme.primary (navy on light / gold on dark) via NavigationBarItemDefaults.colors + primaryContainer indicator. Map tab wraps its icon in BadgedBox+Badge showing GeotagStore.loadAll(context).size (re-read on each backStackEntry change, hidden when 0). Verified on emulator: Camera selected = gold accent + bold; Map tab shows red '7' badge (7 location-tagged photos).

userStories.title: Status-bar padding + low-GPS guidance
userStories.description: As a user, I want no UI under the status bar and GPS hints.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply statusBarsPadding so content isn't under clock/battery
Accuracy > 20m: GPS card turns amber/red + 'Move to open sky for better GPS' hint
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify padding + low-accuracy hint on emulator
userStories.notes: statusBarsPadding was already applied to the GPS-card overlay in CameraPreview (TopCenter) — verified the card renders below the clock/battery. Added low-GPS guidance in LocationInfoOverlay: when fix accuracy > 20m the card background turns the accuracyColor (red Poor at >20m) instead of black, and a 4th line shows a Warning icon + 'Move to open sky for better GPS' hint (string gps_low_accuracy_hint in both values + values-hi). Verified on emulator: at the real ±5m the card stays black with no hint; with a temporarily-lowered threshold the card tinted to accuracyColor + the Hindi hint 'बेहतर GPS के लिए खुले आसमान में जाएँ' rendered (then reverted to >20f).

userStories.title: Weather on the stamp
userStories.description: As a user, I want weather on my stamp.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetch current weather (temp C + condition) via Open-Meteo (no API key) for the coords
Add weather to location data and render on the stamp; graceful offline fallback
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify weather in overlay + saved image on emulator
userStories.notes: New location/WeatherProvider.kt: Weather(temperatureC, weatherCode) + fetchWeather(lat,lng) hits Open-Meteo current_weather (no API key) via HttpURLConnection on Dispatchers.IO with an 8s timeout, parses with org.json, returns null on any failure (offline/HTTP/parse) — same graceful contract as OsmStaticMapProvider. WMO weathercode mapped to coarse condition strings (clear/cloudy/fog/drizzle/rain/snow/showers/thunderstorm/unknown). Weather.describe(context)='28°C · Clear' via weather_stamp string. LocationUiState.Available gained weather:Weather?=null; rememberCurrentLocation fetches weather after geocode and folds it in. Overlay shows a WbSunny icon + describe() line (between plus code and coords). StampData gained weather:String? (pre-formatted, snapshotted at shutter via available.weather.describe(context)); all 3 templates render it (Classic/Minimal after coords, Field Report as a WEATHER labelled row). New weather_* strings in both values + values-hi. INTERNET perm already present. Verified on emulator: overlay shows '☀ 34°C · साफ़'; captured Field Report stamp burns 'WEATHER 34°C · साफ़'.

userStories.title: Camera layout presets
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose which fields appear on the stamp.
acceptanceCriteria: Preset selector: Map+Address+Weather, Map+Lat/Lng+Weather, Map+Address, Address+Weather, Address, Lat/Lng
Selection persists and controls what the stamp renders
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify switching presets changes the saved stamp on emulator
userStories.notes: New LayoutPreset enum in settings/AppSettings.kt: 6 presets (MAP_ADDRESS_WEATHER default / MAP_LATLNG_WEATHER / MAP_ADDRESS / ADDRESS_WEATHER / ADDRESS / LATLNG), each a combo of boolean showMap/showAddress/showCoords/showWeather flags + labelRes. Persisted via AppSettingsStore.load/saveLayoutPreset (same SharedPreferences pattern as Coordinate/Time format). Snapshotted into StampData.layoutPreset at shutter in CameraPreview. PhotoStamp: drawStamp computes effectiveMap = if(showMap) mapThumbnail else null; each template (Classic/Minimal/Field Report) gates its address/coords/weather lines on the flags; project/site/note/logo + date-time always render. PhotoStorage also gates the (network) map fetch on showMap (composes with template.usesMap). Settings gained a 'Stamp layout' radio section (OptionRow per preset). New preset_* + settings_layout_preset(_summary) strings in both values + values-hi. Verified on emulator: picked Lat/Lng preset → Field Report capture burned ONLY PROJECT/SITE + COORDINATES (DMS ±5m) + DATE/TIME rows — no map, no ADDRESS, no WEATHER.

userStories.title: WYSIWYG stamp position
userStories.description: As a user, I want the preview to match the final photo.
acceptanceCriteria: On-screen stamp preview position matches the final burned stamp
Position option (bottom/top) in settings; persists
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify preview matches saved photo position on emulator
userStories.notes: New StampPosition enum (BOTTOM default / TOP) in settings/AppSettings.kt + AppSettingsStore.load/saveStampPosition (same SharedPreferences pattern as the other format prefs). StampData.stampPosition snapshotted at shutter in CameraPreview. PhotoStamp: drawClassic/drawMinimal/drawFieldReport now compute panelHeight then panelTop = if(top) 0f else height-panelHeight (header band/map/lines are panelTop-relative so both edges work). WYSIWYG: CameraPreview reads the position once (remember at tab entry; NavHost re-composes the camera tab on return so a Settings change applies) and places the live LocationInfoOverlay GPS card at the SAME edge — TopCenter when TOP, or as the first child of the bottom controls Column (above the mode chips) when BOTTOM — so the on-screen preview matches the burned photo. Settings gained a 'Stamp position' radio section. New settings_stamp_position(_summary) + position_top/position_bottom strings in both values + values-hi. Verified on emulator: BOTTOM → on-screen card sits above the mode chips + Field Report stamp burns at the BOTTOM; switched to TOP → card moves to the top + the same stamp burns at the TOP (±5m), preview==photo at both edges.

userStories.title: Viewfinder overlays (date/time, altitude, grid)
userStories.description: As a user, I want quick on-screen info and a framing grid.
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom-left: date/time + altitude, 12sp white with shadow
3x3 grid lines (30% white) toggle from Settings
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify overlays + grid toggle on emulator
userStories.notes: Viewfinder overlays + grid. GpsFix gained altitudeMeters (nullable, from Location.hasAltitude()/.altitude in rememberCurrentLocation). New AppSettingsStore.load/saveShowGrid (plain boolean, viewfinder-only, NOT a StampData field). CameraPreview: RuleOfThirdsGrid (Canvas, 2 vertical + 2 horizontal lines at thirds, white@0.3, gated on showGrid read once via remember at tab entry) over the preview; ViewfinderInfoOverlay = bottom-left HUD (first left-aligned child of the bottom controls Column so it never overlaps controls) with a live-ticking date/time line (LaunchedEffect + delay(1000), TimeFormat.datePattern) + an Altitude: Nm line when the fix reports altitude — 12sp white with a black Shadow. Settings: new 'Framing grid' section with a ToggleRow (label + Material3 Switch) inside an OptionCard. New strings settings_grid(_summary/_toggle) + viewfinder_altitude in both values + values-hi. Verified on emulator: enabled grid in Settings → Camera shows the 3x3 grid lines + bottom-left HUD '16 जून 2026 12:48:56 pm IST' / 'ऊँचाई: 342मी' (geo fix altitude 342).

userStories.title: Altitude + compass direction
userStories.description: As a field user, I want altitude and facing direction.
acceptanceCriteria: Capture altitude (m) and compass bearing mapped to cardinal (e.g. NE)
Show on stamp/overlay, e.g. 'Altitude 342m - Facing NE'
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify altitude + direction render on emulator
userStories.notes: New location/CompassProvider.kt: rememberCompassBearing() subscribes to TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR (SensorManager.getRotationMatrixFromVector + getOrientation, azimuth radians→0..360 degrees) via DisposableEffect, emits Float? (null when no sensor — graceful-null contract, no permission). bearingToCardinalRes(deg) maps to 8 cardinal string res (compass_n..nw). formatAltitudeFacing(context, altitude, bearing) builds 'Altitude 342m · Facing NE' from whichever pieces exist (null if neither). Altitude already on GpsFix (US-012). StampData.altitudeFacing (pre-formatted, snapshotted at shutter); rendered in all 3 templates (Classic/Minimal after weather, Field Report as ALTITUDE / FACING labelled row) — always shown when present, NOT gated by layout preset. ViewfinderInfoOverlay HUD now shows the same combined line (replaced the altitude-only line). New compass_facing + stamp_altitude + compass_n/ne/e/se/s/sw/w/nw strings (both values + values-hi; Hindi cardinal abbrevs उ/उपू/पू/दपू/द/दप/प/उप, 'Facing %s'→'%s की ओर'). Verified on emulator: HUD shows 'ऊँचाई 342मी · उ की ओर'; Field Report capture burned an ALTITUDE / FACING row 'ऊँचाई 342मी · उ की ओर' between COORDINATES and DATE/TIME (altitude 342 from geo fix, compass N).

userStories.title: EXIF GPS into the saved file
userStories.description: As a field user, I want machine-readable GPS in the file.
acceptanceCriteria: Write EXIF GPS (lat/long, timestamp, altitude) into the saved JPEG
Tags verifiable with an EXIF reader
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify EXIF GPS on a captured file on emulator
userStories.notes: Added androidx.exifinterface:exifinterface 1.3.7 (catalog + build.gradle). StampData gained raw altitudeMeters:Double? (snapshotted at shutter from available.fix.altitudeMeters, separate from the pre-formatted altitudeFacing line). PhotoStorage.writeGpsExif(context, uri, stamp): after compressing the stamped JPEG (and before clearing IS_PENDING on Q+), opens the MediaStore uri with a 'rw' file descriptor and writes EXIF via androidx ExifInterface — setLatLong + setAltitude, TAG_GPS_DATESTAMP/TIMESTAMP (UTC, rational h/1,m/1,s/1) when a fix exists, plus TAG_DATETIME/_ORIGINAL/_DIGITIZED always. Wrapped in runCatching so an EXIF failure never discards the photo (stamp is still burned in). Verified on emulator: captured GPS_20260616_130644592.jpg, PIL read GPSLatitude 30°7'55.3"N (30.13203), GPSLongitude 78°19'17.2"E (78.32144), GPSAltitude 342.0 (ref above-sea-level), GPSDateStamp 2026:06:16, DateTimeOriginal 2026:06:16 13:06:44.

userStories.title: Home dashboard screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want a home with quick tiles.
acceptanceCriteria: New Home launch destination with tiles: Camera, Gallery, Map, Reports
Clean navy+gold design; tiles navigate to the right screens
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify home tiles + navigation on emulator
userStories.notes: New Home dashboard as the launch destination. Added Destination.Home (Icons.Home, route 'home') as the FIRST entry in the Destination enum + START_DESTINATION=Home (Destinations.kt). New ui/screens/HomeScreen.kt: navy vertical-gradient (BrandNavy→BrandNavyDeep) landing — gold app_name title + Hindi-localized home_subtitle + a 2x2 grid of HomeTileCard (Material3 Card(onClick), aspectRatio 1, BrandNavySurface bg, gold icon in a BrandNavyLight circle + white label). Tiles: Camera(PhotoCamera)/Gallery(PhotoLibrary)/Map(Map)/Reports(PictureAsPdf); Reports routes to Gallery.route (PDF report export lives there, US-017). HomeScreen takes onTileClick:(route)->Unit (no NavController dep). MainActivity: NavHost gains the Home composable; extracted NavHostController.navigateTopLevel (popUpTo start saveState + launchSingleTop + restoreState) reused by BOTH the Home tiles AND the bottom-nav onClick. New strings tab_home/tab_reports/home_subtitle in both values+values-hi. Verified on emulator: Home is the launch screen (gold 'Gps Camera Location' + 4 navy tiles with gold icons), bottom nav shows होम selected (gold home icon); tapping the रिपोर्ट tile navigated to Gallery (गैलरी gold-selected in nav).

userStories.title: Photo Grid / collage
userStories.description: As a user, I want to combine photos into a grid image.
acceptanceCriteria: Select 2-4 photos in Gallery and arrange in a grid (2x2, side-by-side)
Export the grid as one shareable image; each photo keeps its stamp
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify grid creation + export on emulator
userStories.notes: New media/PhotoCollage.kt: createPhotoCollage(context, photos) combines 2-4 (COLLAGE_MIN/MAX_PHOTOS) selected photos into ONE 1200px-wide grid image. Layout = 2 columns, rows=ceil(n/2): 2 photos sit side-by-side, 4 form a 2x2, 3 = 2+1. Each photo is fit-inside (contain, never cropped) its square cell on a white bg so EVERY photo's burned-in stamp is preserved (verified: 3-photo collage kept all 3 full FIELD REPORT stamps). decodeScaledToMax downsamples each to ~2x cell. Saved as GPS_collage_<ts>.jpg into Pictures/gpstools via the same scoped-storage saveBitmap pattern (keeps the GPS_ prefix so it shows in the in-app gallery). shareImage(context,uri,title) fires a generic ACTION_SEND image share. GalleryScreen: GalleryActionBar gained a 'ग्रिड बनाएँ'/Make grid TextButton (GridView icon) next to Export PDF; createCollageSelected() validates 2-4 selection (else toast collage_invalid_count), shows the generating overlay (generatingLabel state, reused by both PDF+collage), creates off-main-thread, toasts collage_saved, refreshes the grid, and fires the share sheet. New collage_* strings in both values + values-hi. Verified on emulator-5554: selected 3 photos → Make grid → 'Creating grid…' overlay → share sheet 'Sharing image' with the 2x2 preview; pulled GPS_collage_20260616_132843.jpg (1200x1200 JPEG), all 3 FIELD REPORT stamps intact, 4th cell white.

userStories.title: Timestamp toggle + custom watermark
userStories.description: As a user, I want to toggle the timestamp and add my watermark.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings: date/time ON/OFF on the stamp
Custom watermark (company name/logo) renders bottom-right of the stamp
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify toggle + watermark on saved image on emulator

userStories.title: Pro gestures (timer + swipe)
userStories.description: As a power user, I want quick shutter shortcuts.
acceptanceCriteria: Long-press shutter = 3-sec self-timer with countdown
Swipe left on the viewfinder opens the last photo / gallery preview
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify both gestures on emulator
